title: Calibration & Selective-Prediction Benchmark for Perturbation Prediction (minimal public v1)
thesis: A model-agnostic split-conformal + locally-adaptive trust gate delivers VALID marginal coverage and a useful risk-coverage tradeoff regardless of which perturbation predictor it wraps, across TWO independent datasets. This is a calibration/selective-prediction eval, NOT an accuracy leaderboard.

datasets.name: Gladstone_CD4T_T1D
datasets.target: log mean expression of target gene

datasets.name: Norman2019_K562
datasets.split: repeated random calib/test split 50/50, 200 repeats (n=105, SMALL — flagged)

datasets.name: Datlinger2017_Jurkat_Tcell
datasets.split: repeated random calib/test 50/50, 200 repeats (n=64: 32 perts x 2 conditions — SMALL, flagged)
datasets.feature_note: log_n_cells DROPPED: corr -0.95 with y is a target-construction artifact (Welch z ~ 1/sqrt(n_cells)), not signal — leakage at small n.

predictors.desc: predict training-mean of y (trivial control; MUST still calibrate)
Ridge regression on features (MANDATORY simple baseline, per Ahlmann-Eltze 2025 Nat Methods)

GEARS-STYLE relational predictor: held-out perturbation effect = co-expression-similarity-weighted avg of RELATED training perturbations; similarity from target-gene co-expression in CONTROL cells (leakage-free); leave-one-PERTURBATION-out. NOT GEARS itself (no GNN/GO-graph/cell-level training; laptop-feasible analog of GEARS' inductive bias). Evaluated on Datlinger (the dataset with full control-cell data).

conformal.marginal: split conformal on |y-pred| calib residuals; q = ceil((n+1)(1-alpha))/n empirical quantile; constant-width interval pred±q. Used for the COVERAGE-VALIDITY axis.
conformal.locally_adaptive: normalized nonconformity |y-pred|/sigma(x), sigma(x)=auxiliary HistGBR fit of |resid|~features on calib (Lei 2018 / Papadopoulos 2008). Per-point width → ranks points for the SELECTIVE-PREDICTION axis.

primary_metrics.coverage_validity: empirical coverage at each nominal level; PASS iff |empirical-nominal| <= 0.03 (marginal) OR within Clopper-Pearson 95% CI. Calibration error = mean_abs(empirical-nominal) across a 19-level sweep (0.05..0.95).
primary_metrics.selective_prediction: risk-coverage curve = RMSE on the (1-abstain) most-confident points (ranked by locally-adaptive sigma(x)) vs retained fraction; AURC = area under it; compare to random-abstention null (mean over 100 shuffles) — PASS iff AURC < random-null AURC.

success_criteria: Calibration VALIDITY holds (within tolerance) for the generic predictors on ALL THREE datasets = model-agnostic claim across contexts. Relational predictor: does the trust gate calibrate a STRUCTURALLY DIFFERENT (relational, not feature-regression) predictor? Report honestly incl. small-n failures.
honesty_guards: claim is calibration-validity + model-agnosticism, NOT SOTA accuracy
metric defs frozen by this prereg hash before running
relational predictor is GEARS-STYLE (inductive bias only), NOT GEARS — stated everywhere
target y identical across all 3 datasets; calibration assessed WITHIN dataset, never by comparing raw y across datasets (different gene universes)
Datlinger log_n_cells dropped as a target-construction confound (documented pre-run)
